reasoning: The baseline data shows that only 52% of Nigerian adults had recognized bank accounts as of 2023, while overall financial inclusion (which includes informal financial services) stood at 74%. The 95% figure was purely a target set by the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN) for the end of 2024, not an actual achievement. A country cannot plausibly double its percentage of banked adults in a single year under existing economic conditions. With reliable CBN and EFInA data confirming a 52% rate just a year prior to the deadline, there is no realistic path for the bank account rate to reach 95% before 2025. Therefore, the probability of encountering data reporting 95% or more consists only of the most extreme reporting errors, justifying a near-zero estimate.
